'''小出氏'''は、日本の氏族の一つ。
小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、発祥の地は[[信濃国|信濃]]伊那とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、江戸時代も大名として存続した。
{{japanese-history-stub|こいてし}}
[[Category:小出氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族|こいてし]]

revision.text:
{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋= |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋= |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋= |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]

[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

revision.text:
{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋= |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|丸に額 |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]

[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/koide_k.html 小出氏系譜] *[http://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/kp/koto/tanba/127.html 園部藩14代当主] {{Commons|Category:Koide Clan}} {{japanese-history-stub|こいてし}} [[Category:小出氏|*]] [[Category:日本の氏族|こいてし]]

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|丸に額 |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷，額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] 小出氏の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|丸に額 |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]

[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷，[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の家紋は'''小出氏本家'''が'''丸に額'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''額に二八'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''亀甲に小の字'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|丸に額 |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|100px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の家紋は'''小出氏本家'''が'''丸に額'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''額に二八'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''亀甲に小の字'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|100px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の'''家紋'''は'''小出氏本家'''が'''丸に額'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''額に二八'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''亀甲に小の字'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|100px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の'''[[家紋]]'''は'''小出氏本家'''が'''丸に額'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''額に二八'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''亀甲に小の字'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|100px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の'''[[家紋]]'''は'''小出氏本家'''が'''「丸に額」''''''「丸に額形」、「丸に堅額」'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''「額に二八文字」'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''亀甲に小の字'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。

{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|100px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の'''[[家紋]]'''は'''小出氏本家'''が'''「丸に額」、「丸に額形」、「丸に堅額」'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''「額に二八文字」'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''「亀甲に小の字」'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。
嫡流（出石藩主家）

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/124.132.154.119|124.132.154.119]] ([[User talk:124.132.154.119|会話]]) による ID:56778886 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]] 更なる転載を差し戻し
revision.text:
{{Sakujo/本体|2015年8月27日|小出氏}} {{copyrights}} '''小出氏'''（こいでし）は、日本の氏族の一つ。 {{日本の氏族 |家名=小出氏
（小出氏） |家紋=Mon Gaku.jpg|Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg |家紋名称=丸に額、額に二八文字、亀甲に小の字 |本姓=[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[工藤氏]]流? |家祖=[[小出時氏]][[二階堂氏]]流、[[小出能綱]][[工藤氏]]流? |種別=[[武家]]
[[華族]]（[[子爵]]） |出身地=[[信濃国]][[伊那郡]][[小井弖郷]]（[[小出郷]]） |根拠地=[[信濃国]]、[[越後国]]、[[尾張国]] |人物=[[小出秀政]]
[[小出吉政]]
[[小出秀家]]
[[小出吉英]]
[[小出吉親]]
[[小出英尚]] |支流=[[出石藩]]主家、[[陶器藩]]主家、[[園部藩]]主家（[[武家]]） }} == 小出氏 == [[ファイル:Residenz Koide.jpg|400px|thumb|江戸時代の武家屋敷、[[家紋]]の額紋を掲揚は小出信濃守]] [[ファイル:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|thumb|200px|小出氏分家の家紋、額に二八文字]] '''小出氏'''の出自は[[藤原南家]][[二階堂氏]]流とされ、[[本貫]]の地は[[信濃国|信濃]][[伊那郡]]小井弖郷（小出郷）とされる。[[安土桃山時代]]、[[江戸時代]]に[[大名]]となった小出氏は、上述の小出氏の子孫が[[尾張国|尾張]]中村（現[[名古屋市]]）に流れてきたものとされるが定かではない。[[小出秀政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の母方の縁者として大名に取り立てられ、秀吉没後の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]では西軍に属したが、秀政の一子遠江守[[小出秀家|秀家]]が東軍に属したため[[改易]]を免れ、[[江戸時代]]も大名として存続した。 == 家紋 == この頃の'''[[家紋]]'''は'''小出氏本家'''が'''「丸に額」、「丸に額形」、「丸に堅額」'''を用い、'''小出氏分家'''は'''「額に二八文字」'''を用い、'''替紋'''は'''「亀甲に小の字」'''を用いた。 ==系譜== ;太字は当主、実線は実子、点線は養子。
嫡流（出石藩主家）

revision.text: [[de:Koide (Klan)]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:小出氏]]

revision.text:
[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:小出氏]]

In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:小出氏]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:小出氏]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:小出氏]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Koide clan (Negi Daibu)-qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Koide clan (Negi Daibu)-qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Koide clan (Negi Daibu)-qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Koide clan (Negi Daibu)-qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:kk]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:小出氏]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * revision.text:
[[de:Koide (Klan)]] {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also! == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)}}[[Category:11]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}} }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡|12=draft}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡|12=draft}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=draft}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=draft}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=draft}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)}}

The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡|12=draft}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡|12=draft}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=draft}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=draft}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=draft}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)}}
