The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡|12=draft}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡|12=draft}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=draft}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=draft}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=draft}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|12=draft}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)}}

The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|qq=draft}} until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Ina District, Nagano|ja|上伊那郡|qq=draft}} of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡|qq=draft}}, members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft}} with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft}}. By 1595, the family was relocated to {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft}}, receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|qq=draft}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Koide clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]]

Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed {{ill|Kishiwada Domain|ja|岸和田藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishiwada Domain|qq=}} and {{ill|Sonobe Domain|ja|園部藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonobe Domain|qq=}}. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]]

{{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|qq=}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]]

By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:上伊那郡]] [[Category:中部地方の氏族]] [[Category:伊那市の歴史]] [[Category:小出氏]]

{{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|qq=}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:上伊那郡]] [[Category:中部地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:小出氏]]

Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Viscount clans of the Kazoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:上伊那郡]] [[Category:中部地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Buke (Adel)]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:上伊那郡]] [[Category:中部地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:武家の氏族]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

{{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|qq=}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:上伊那郡]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|qq=}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[sn:Koide clan (Negi Daibu)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|qq=|1=Tōki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10863472}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|qq=|1=koidemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459106}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|qq=|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|qq=|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856352}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}}

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}}

[[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|1=Tōki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10863472}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|1=koidemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459106}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856352}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}}

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|1=Tōki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10863472}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|1=koidemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459106}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856352}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] [[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|1=Tōki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10863472}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|1=koidemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459106}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856352}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] [[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] [[de:Koide (Klan)]][[ja:小出氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11459112]] Wikidata name: Koide clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=May 2017}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | Name = Koide Clan (小出氏) | Mon = [[File:Mon Gaku.jpg|50px]] [[File:Mon Gaku 2-8.jpg|50px]] | Mon description = Maruni-Gaku, Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji, Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji | Founder = [[Koide Tokiuji]] (Nikaido lineage) or [[Koide Yoshitsuna]] (Kudo lineage) | Home Province = [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] | Prominent members = [[Koide Hideyasu]]
[[Koide Yoshimasa]]
[[Koide Hideie]]
[[Koide Yoshiie]]
[[Koide Yoshichika]]
[[Koide Hidenao]] | Ruled until = Abolition of the han system in 1871 | Titles = Daimyo, Viscount (Kazoku) | Dissolution = | Cadet Branches = Lords of [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], {{ill|Toki Domain|ja|陶器藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki Domain|1=Tōki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10863472}}, and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] }} '''Koide Clan''' (小出氏) was a Japanese samurai and noble family. The clan rose to prominence during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], becoming a daimyo family. They ruled the [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] in [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] until the abolition of the feudal domain system during the [[Meiji Restoration]], after which they were ennobled as Viscounts in the [[Kazoku]] peerage system. == Origins == The Koide Clan claims descent from the [[:en:Nikaido clan|Nikaido clan]] of the [[Southern Fujiwara]] lineage, tracing their roots to {{ill|Koide Village|ja|小出村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Village|1=koidemura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459106}} in the [[:en:Kamiina District, Nagano|Ina District, Nagano]] of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]], members of the clan served in various roles for the Kamakura shogunate, including ceremonial horse-handlers. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}. The clan's prominence began with [[Koide Hideyasu]], who became the husband of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s maternal aunt, securing favor and territory under the Toyotomi regime. In 1585, Hideyasu was granted [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] with a stipend of 30,000 koku. == History as Daimyo == The Koide family continued to serve under the Toyotomi and later the Tokugawa regimes. Hideyasu's son, [[Koide Yoshimasa]], was granted {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856352}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. By 1595, the family was relocated to [[:en:Izushi Domain|Izushi Domain]], receiving 60,000 koku. Despite siding with the Western Army during the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they avoided confiscation of their domain thanks to Yoshimasa's younger brother, who fought for the Eastern Army. Over time, the Koide family's holdings were redistributed, and different branches governed [[:en:Kishiwada Domain|Kishiwada Domain]] and [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]]. The Sonobe branch persisted until the Meiji Restoration. == Koide Clan as Kazoku == After the Meiji Restoration, the head of the Sonobe branch, [[Koide Hidenao]], was appointed governor of Sonobe Domain. In 1884, his descendant [[Koide Hidefumi]] was ennobled as a Viscount under the Kazoku system. The family maintained their status as part of the Japanese nobility into the early 20th century. == Family Crest == The Koide clan used the crests "Maruni-Gaku" (circle with a framed pattern) and "Gaku-ni-Nihachi-moji" (framed pattern with the characters for two and eight), along with an alternative crest, "Kikko-ni-Ko-no-ji" (hexagon with the character for 'Ko'). == Notable Members == * [[Koide Hideyasu]] – First daimyo of Kishiwada Castle * [[Koide Yoshimasa]] – Daimyo of Tatsuno and Izushi * [[Koide Hidenao]] – Last daimyo of Sonobe Domain == References == * {{Cite book|author=Ota Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kanko Kai|year=1934}} * {{Cite book|author=Kanzan Nitta|title=Nihon Jinbutsu Shiso|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=1984}} * {{Cite book|author=Yuji Odabe|title=Kazoku no Jitsuzo|publisher=Chuko Shinsho|year=2006}} * {{Cite book|author=Masaomi Asami|title=Kizoku Tanjyo|publisher=Libroport|year=1994}} == See Also == * [[Japanese clans]] * [[:en:Kishiwada Castle|Kishiwada Castle]] * [[:en:Sonobe Domain|Sonobe Domain]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小出氏|version=101122139|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Koide (Klan)]] [[ja:小出氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11459112}} [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:History of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamiina District]] [[Category:Koide Clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Other sources, such as the {{ill|Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja|百家系図稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakka Keizu Ko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggest a connection to the {{ill|Kudo clan|ja|工藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kudo clan|1=Kudō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479597}}.
